items.title: [security] Built-in secret redaction at jsonl write-time in ~/.claude/projects/
items.desc:
## Summary

Claude Code persists every sub-agent transcript to `~/.claude/projects/<id>/*.jsonl` and records tool stdout **verbatim**. If a shell command echoes a secret (`echo \$API_KEY`, `gcloud iam service-accounts keys create out.json`, `cat creds.env`, env-var dumps from `printenv`, etc.), the secret is now durably on disk in the session transcript with no built-in redaction.

This becomes a leak vector when:

- the transcript dir is checked into git (a config-sync repo or dotfile mirror)
-...
items.llm_title: Claude Code 会话日志敏感信息泄露
items.llm_summary: Claude Code 将子代理的完整会话记录明文存储到磁盘文件中，包括工具的标准输出。如果执行的shell命令（如环境变量输出、密钥生成）输出了API密钥、凭证等敏感信息，这些信息会原样被持久化，导致本地信息泄露。当会话目录被版本控制（如git）同步时，泄露风险进一步放大。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 使用Claude Code执行一个会泄露敏感信息的shell命令，例如：`echo $API_KEY` 或 `printenv`。
2. 检查会话记录目录 `~/.claude/projects/<id>/` 下的 `.jsonl` 文件。
3. 在文件中会看到明文存储的、包含密钥或敏感环境变量的命令输出内容。

items.title: Security: settings.local.json permissions file has no integrity protection — silent injection enables pre-approved execution
items.desc:
## Summary

The `settings.local.json` file that controls which tool calls execute without user confirmation has no integrity protection. Any process running as the current user can silently inject permission entries, pre-approving arbitrary command execution. Claude Code will then execute those commands without prompting.

## Attack Surface

The permissions file lives at predictable paths:
- `<project>/.claude/settings.local.json` (project-level)
- `~/.claude/settings.json` (global)

Both are pl...

items.title: fix(security): add ReDoS guard to exec approval argPattern matching
items.desc:
## Problem

`matchArgPattern` in `src/infra/exec-command-resolution.ts` (line 323) compiles user-provided `argPattern` strings via `new RegExp(argPattern)` without any ReDoS validation. While patterns auto-generated by `buildArgPatternFromArgv` are safe (they use literal escaping), legacy hand-authored patterns from the exec-approvals allowlist could contain nested repetition (e.g., `(a+)+$`) that causes catastrophic backtracking.

## Fix

Add a `hasNestedRepetition(argPattern)` guard before `ne...

items.title: [Security] Ship a dedicated/bundled Node binary so macOS TCC permissions (FDA, Accessibility) are scoped to OpenClaw only
items.desc:
## Problem

When users grant **Full Disk Access** (or Accessibility) to OpenClaw on macOS, they must grant it to the shared `node` binary (e.g. `/usr/local/bin/node` or an nvm-managed path). macOS TCC has no mechanism to scope that permission to OpenClaw specifically — the grant applies to **every process that runs through the same binary**, including:

- All globally installed npm packages
- All `node_modules` in any project on the machine
- Any supply-chain-compromised dependency

items.title: feat: add reversible sensitive data sanitization layer (Security Guardrail)
items.desc:
## Core Value / 核心价值

OpenClaw 已经能承担非常多有意义的工作——自动化运维、代码生成、数据查询、多轮对话协作。但随着使用场景深入，一个安全顾虑始终制约着更多用户的探索：**既想用公有云模型的强大能力，又担心对话中不可避免地暴露数据库连接串、API Key、内网地址等敏感信息。**

这个 PR 提供了一个务实的解决方案：用本地模型做智能语义脱敏，敏感数据不出本地网络，云端模型全程只看到 `[VAULT_N]` 占位符，回程自动还原——用户无感，安全无忧。让团队能放心地把 OpenClaw + 公有模型用到生产级场景中，不必在便利和安全之间做取舍。

OpenClaw already handles a wide range of meaningful work — automated ops, code generation, data queries, multi-turn collaboration. But as usage deepens, a persistent security concern holds back broader adopt...

allow_scripts = true

Previous hardening covered unsafe `inbox/<messageId>` directories and file-path targets, but both channel-inbound attachment extraction and agent-to-agent file forwarding still trusted the top-level session `inbox` directory as the containment root. Because the session directory is writable from the agent container, a compromised or prompt-injected agent could re...
items.llm_title: NanoClaw 会话收件箱符号链接沙箱逃逸
items.llm_summary: NanoClaw的会话管理器在处理入站附件时，未对外部代理容器可写的顶层会话`inbox`目录进行充分的路径解析验证。攻击者（或被注入的代理）可在此目录创建符号链接指向主机上的任意路径，从而在后续的附件提取或文件转发操作中绕过沙箱限制，读写主机文件系统。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 作为攻击者控制或提示注入一个代理容器。
2. 在容器内，向共享的会话`inbox`根目录写入一个符号链接，例如 `ln -s /host/secret.txt /inbox/link`。
3. 触发一项操作（如发送带有附件的消息或代理间文件转发），该操作会基于`inbox`目录提取文件。
4. 系统在解析路径时跟随了符号链接，导致操作被重定向到主机上的`/host/secret.txt`，而非预期的沙箱内路径。

items.llm_title: NanoClaw CLI批准命令调用上下文恢复
items.llm_summary: NanoClaw的CLI命令在需要管理员批准的流程中，重放批准后的命令时会丢失原始调用上下文（如来自哪个代理或用户），并强制以`host`身份执行。这可能导致权限提升或身份混淆，使得本应由低权限代理执行的操作，错误地以高权限主机身份完成。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 一个代理（非主机）发起一个需要批准的敏感CLI命令。
2. 管理员在审批界面批准了该请求。
3. 系统重放此命令时，未使用原始代理的上下文，而是以`host`身份执行。
4. 这使得原本权限不足的代理，通过批准流程间接触发了高权限操作，构成权限绕过。

Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10)  // ~41 bits of entropy

This id **is the secret** for click approvals. The Chat SDK's Telegram adapter uses it dir...
items.llm_title: NanoClaw 审批卡ID弱随机数修复
items.llm_summary: NanoClaw的审批卡片流程中，使用`Math.random()`生成代表审批机密的ID，其熵值仅约41位。该ID被用作外部服务（如Telegram）交互的令牌，弱随机性使得攻击者可能预测或暴力破解该令牌，从而伪造或拦截审批操作，绕过安全控制。
items.llm_reproduction:

2. 基于`Math.random()`的可预测性（或已知种子），进行大量计算预测下一个可能的ID。
3. 在审批流程中，使用预测的ID构造请求。
4. 如果预测成功，可绕过正常的审批流程执行未授权操作。

MCP HTTP/SSE connection setup can attempt a loopback connection before rejecting private or loopback targets. This means MCP configured URLs do not consistently apply the same SSRF validation expected from network-facing fetch tools.

## Root cause

The MCP HTTP/SSE setup path accepts or probes configured URLs before applying a private-network/loopback rejection.

## Failure scenario

1. Configure an MCP HTTP/SSE server URL such as `http://127.0.0.1:9/sse`.
2. Initialize/probe the MC...
items.llm_title: MCP 服务器SSRF防护逻辑缺陷

items.llm_reproduction:
1. 将MCP服务器的HTTP/SSE URL配置为指向内网地址（如`http://127.0.0.1:9/sse`）。
2. 在代理初始化或探测该MCP配置时，系统会先尝试连接该内网地址。
3. 尽管最终连接可能被拒绝，但请求已被发出，可能泄露信息或触发内部服务响应。

3. The request succeeds and calls `process_direct`.

items.llm_title: AI代理工作目录限制可绕过执行命令

items.llm_reproduction: 原文说明当前实现允许通过传递一个在工作区外的`working_dir`来绕过路径检查。复现方法是在工具调用中设置`working_dir`为系统敏感路径（如`/`）并执行命令。

items.title: [Security] Filesystem tools do not enforce restrict_to_workspace
items.desc:
## Description
Currently, the filesystem tools (`ReadFileTool`, `WriteFileTool`, `EditFileTool`, and `ListDirTool`) in `nanobot/agent/tools/filesystem.py` do not enforce the `restrict_to_workspace` configuration. 

While the shell execution tool may have safety guards, these direct file tools can still access, read, or write files anywhere on the host system (e.g., `~/.ssh/id_rsa` or `~/.nanobot/config.json`). This poses a significant security risk for users who expect the agent to be sandboxed ...

items.title: [Security] Stop unwrapping SecretString into String on the credential path
items.desc:
Follow-up from Zaki's approval on #3256. `secrecy = "0.10"` is already a dependency, but `ResolvedHostCredential.secret_value` is a plain `String` and the construction site at `src/tools/wasm/wrapper.rs:2803` actively unwraps a `SecretString` into one (`secret.expose().to_string()`). After that point the credential sits on the heap without zeroing on drop.

The signer parse fns (`parse_secp256k1_secret`, `parse_ed25519_secret` around `wrapper.rs:655-820`) also decode key material into `Vec<u8>` ...

items.title: [Security] Signer approval gate should not be Optional
items.desc:
Follow-up from Zaki's approval on #3256. The per-call signer gate is wired in production but the field is `Option<Arc<dyn SignerApprovalGate>>` and `gate_signing_if_required` short-circuits with `Ok(())` if no gate is installed (`src/tools/wasm/wrapper.rs:566`). Safe today because production sets `TerminalSignerGate`, but a future caller that constructs the registry without `with_signer_gate(...)` will silently sign High-classification operations.

Same Optional pattern lives on `ToolRegistry` (...

items.title: [Security] Wallet signing requires an unforgeable user-authorization channel, not host-resident keys
items.desc:
## Context

PR #3256 adds host-resident credential signers for HMAC, EIP-712, NEP-413, and Solana. The cryptographic implementations are sound — keys never cross the WASM boundary, manifest-bound EIP-712 domains, correct NEP-413 prefix, tight input validation. The architectural problem is independent of the crypto: **the signing key lives in the IronClaw host process and signs silently on every invocation once a tool's capability is approved at install time.**

This issue tracks the architectura...

items.title: feat(security): add tirith pre-exec scanning on interactive shell approval paths
items.desc:
### What is Tirith?

[Tirith](https://github.com/sheeki03/tirith) is a terminal-security CLI written in Rust that analyzes shell commands for content-level threats that regex deny-lists do not reliably catch:

- **Homograph / punycode attacks** — lookalike domains using Unicode confusables (Cyrillic / Greek / Fullwidth), mixed-script TLDs, and IDN spoofing
- **Pipe-to-interpreter patterns** — `curl ... | bash`, `wget ... | sh`, PowerShell `iwr | iex`, and wrapper variants with `sudo` / `env...

items.title: security(webhooks): add HMAC verification
items.desc:
Addresses #1507 by tightening the public webhook trigger endpoint:

- prefer X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC verification over the legacy X-Webhook-Secret header
- return a generic auth failure for webhook auth errors to avoid path/config enumeration
- update web gateway docs and feature parity notes

Validation:
- cargo fmt --all -- --check
- cargo clippy -p ironclaw --lib --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p ironclaw --lib webhooks::tests -- --nocapture

This report was produced from a hands-on review of the codebase and a series ...

items.title: [GHSA] nono: Sandbox escape on Linux via D-Bus: `systemd-run --user`
items.desc:
### Summary

The nono Landlock/seccomp policies allow access to local Unix domain sockets (concrete and abstract). This allows an easy sandbox escape by talking to the per-user systemd dbus socket.

Threat scenario: Running Aider, Claude Code, OpenCode or similar tools with "allow bash" policy so that it can invoke arbitrary host tools like `make`, `gcc`, etc. to write code.

### Reproducer

Here, instead of running a tool like `opencode` or `claude` one can just invoke `systemd-run`, but this i...
items.llm_title: Claude Code Linux D-Bus沙箱逃逸
items.llm_summary: Aider、Claude Code等工具使用的非安全沙箱策略因过度放通Unix域socket访问，导致攻击者可通过与systemd的D-Bus通信绕过沙箱，在主机上执行任意命令，实现沙箱逃逸。
items.llm_reproduction: 1. 在允许执行主机工具（如bash）的策略下运行Claude Code等工具。2. 攻击者利用漏洞，不通过工具而是直接与systemd用户D-Bus套接字通信，执行如`systemd-run`等命令。3. 通过此方式在主机上获得未经授权的命令执行权限，从而逃逸出沙箱环境。

These tools don’t just suggest code they can read local files and run shell commands. That’s very powerful, but it also means a prompt injection (or poisoned context) can turn a “helpful assistant” into something that looks a lot like an attacker’s shell.

I noticed that Cursor has publicly patched prompt-injection issues, including ones that opened paths to...

items.title: AI agent has more production access than your senior engineers
items.desc:
There’s a pattern I keep seeing across teams adopting AI agents for infrastructure, and it’s starting to scare me.

A team sets up an agent to help with Terraform, Kubernetes configs, or cloud deployments. To make it useful, they give it cloud credentials, state files, sometimes CI/CD pipeline access. They test it in staging. It works. They move it to production. Nobody asks: what’s the blast radius if this thing gets prompt-injected?

Coding agents are not infrastructure agents.

The in...

The problem: agents need file access to work, but if they can read files, they can read their own credentials. One prompt injection and your API keys are leaked.

items.llm_title: AI代理敏感配置文件读取导致凭据泄露
items.llm_summary: 该条目揭示了AI编程代理（如OpenClaw、Claude Desktop）的一项常见安全风险：代理本身可能被诱导读取其自身环境（如.env文件、环境变量）中的敏感凭据（API密钥等）。这是一个明确的“自我信息泄露”风险，攻击者可通过提示注入（如让代理“检查配置”）轻松窃取这些密钥。
items.llm_reproduction: 1. 启动一个具有文件系统访问权限的AI代理（如Claude Desktop或配置了文件操作工具的代理）。 2. 向代理发送一个看似合理的指令，例如：“检查当前环境的配置”或“读取项目根目录的.env文件”。 3. 观察代理的回复，它可能会直接输出包含API密钥、数据库密码等敏感信息的配置文件内容。
